title: The Bigger the Better
description: Using grocery store data, I measured how price changes with product weight. By binning weights and taking the mean price of each bin, I was able to compare price fluctuations between different stores for largely similar products.

title: Heart Disease
description: Through a Bayesian network, we were able to predict the presence of heart disease within a patient using health factors such as heartrate, blood pressure, and blood sugar with high accuracy.

title: Exploring the Relationship Between the Calories and Rating of a Recipe
description: By analyzing the nutritional data of various foods on recipe.com, I found that recipes with less calories tend to receive higher ratings. I also built a prediction model that classified recipe ratings according to its nutritional and instructional info.

title: Nutritional Differences Between Plant-Based and Animal Burger Patties
description: We collected data about various plant-based and animal patties from different brands and compared variability in nutritional values such as calories, saturated fat, and sodium.
